audience: AI product builders evaluating whether a design-to-production workflow can become a reliable product system.
Design engineering leaders responsible for repeatable native presentation quality.
Technical founders deciding whether AI deck generation can support launch and customer proof.
business_job: Explain product-system learning through source data, selected memory, workflow policy, and native PPT generation.
Show not one-shot prompting by proving how the same contract compounds into repeatable output quality.
business_decision: Decide whether the platform is credible enough to use for a public launch, investor demo, or design-engineering customer briefing instead of ad hoc presentation prompting.
deck_mission: Turn the Run 2.7 data, memory, and workflow contract into a six-slide launch narrative that proves editable PPT output can be controlled by traceable design memory.

six_slide_arc.job: Name the product-system promise with one launch claim and one native editable visual field.
must_show: One native editable visual field that signals product-system learning.
must_not_show: Source brand marks, copied launch visuals, or rasterized title treatment.

six_slide_arc.job: Show why AI deck generation fails when each deck is treated as a one-off prompt.
must_show: The workflow problem created by one-shot prompting.
A clear setup for why product-system learning is needed.
must_not_show: Generic AI feature claims or a prompt-tip tutorial.

six_slide_arc.job: Contrast prompt-only output with a workflow that selects source records and design memory before generation.
must_show: Prompt-only output contrasted with source-record and design-memory selection.
A visible workflow choice before generation.
must_not_show: Equal-weight dashboard cards that hide the contrast.

six_slide_arc.job: Trace multimodal source observations into deterministic typography, spacing, composition, and rhythm memory.
must_show: Source records becoming deterministic design memory.
Typography, spacing, composition, and rhythm rules as traceable inputs.
must_not_show: Opaque before/after output without memory ids or trace fields.

six_slide_arc.job: Reveal one large native proof object that makes the design-to-production improvement visible.
must_show: One large native editable proof object.
The visible improvement from design memory to generated PPT structure.
must_not_show: Full-slide raster proof, copied UI, or source-like demo frame.

six_slide_arc.job: Close on the launch decision, editable PPT trace, and public_blocked release gate.
must_show: Editable PPT trace and public_blocked release gate.
must_not_show: Any claim that public release is approved before native render and human review.

must_show: Data source records become explicit design rules before generation.
Design memory is selected by slide rhythm role, not invented after generation.
Workflow decisions are traceable through native editable PPT output.
The quality gate blocks public release until contact sheet and native render checks pass.
must_not_show: Do not copy source layouts, media, or prose.
Do not imitate any source brand, product brand, event identity, or logo system.
Do not use full-slide raster output or rasterized text as the generated deck surface.
proof_questions: Which data records shaped this slide?
Which PPT objects remain native, editable, and traceable?
